title: cardamine aquatica
intro: Cardamine aquatica, also known as water cress, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of vitamins and minerals. The health benefits of water cress include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support cardiovascular health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Water cress can be prepared as a tea, added to salads, or used as a compress to harness its medicinal properties.
